repo.name: outline
repo.description: A team knowledge base and collaborative wiki: a real-time, ProseMirror-based document editor backed by a Node/Koa API, Postgres/Sequelize models, Redis-backed queues, and a React/MobX single-page app.

clusters.label: Frontend App Shell
clusters.summary: The React + MobX single-page application that renders the UI, manages client state, and talks to the API.

clusters.label: Document Editing & Collaboration UI
clusters.summary: The shared ProseMirror-based rich-text editor schema plus the client editor that wires it into the app.

clusters.label: API Layer
clusters.summary: The Koa HTTP server and versioned JSON API endpoints, middlewares, presenters, and command/business logic.

clusters.label: Authentication & Access
clusters.summary: Passport-based login, OAuth provider plugins, session middleware, and account provisioning.

clusters.label: Real-time Sync & Background Jobs
clusters.summary: Hocuspocus collaboration server, websocket fan-out, and the Redis-backed queue processors and tasks.

clusters.label: Data & Models
clusters.summary: Sequelize models, database connection, and migrations defining the persisted domain.

clusters.label: Storage & Attachments
clusters.summary: Pluggable file storage (local/S3) for attachments, avatars, imports, and exports.

nodes.label: App Entry & Routing
nodes.summary: Boots the React app, sets up routing, theme, and the global authenticated/public layouts.

nodes.label: Scenes
nodes.summary: Top-level page components for documents, collections, search, settings, and login.

nodes.label: MobX Stores
nodes.summary: Observable client-side state stores that load, cache, and mutate data via the API.

nodes.label: Client Models
nodes.summary: Client-side model classes mirroring server entities and decorated for MobX observability.

nodes.label: UI Components
nodes.summary: Reusable presentational and container React components shared across scenes.

nodes.label: Shared Editor Schema
nodes.summary: The isomorphic ProseMirror schema (nodes, marks, plugins, commands) shared by client and server.

nodes.label: Client Editor
nodes.summary: Wraps the shared editor with client extensions, menus, and components for in-app editing.

nodes.label: Server Entry
nodes.summary: Bootstraps services (web, worker, websockets, collaboration) and starts the Node process.

nodes.label: API Routes
nodes.summary: Versioned JSON endpoints grouped by resource (documents, collections, auth, etc.).

nodes.label: documents.ts
nodes.summary: Implements the documents.* endpoints (create, update, info, search, move, archive).

nodes.label: Middlewares
nodes.summary: Koa middlewares for authentication, rate limiting, CSRF, multipart, and request context.

nodes.label: Commands
nodes.summary: Transactional business-logic operations that mutate domain entities (create, move, import documents).

nodes.label: Presenters
nodes.summary: Serialize Sequelize models into the JSON shapes returned by the API.

nodes.label: Authorization Policies
nodes.summary: Cancan-style ability checks that authorize a user against an action on a model.

nodes.label: Auth Routes
nodes.summary: HTTP routes that mount provider login flows and issue authenticated session cookies.

nodes.label: Passport Strategy
nodes.summary: Shared Passport callback handling that turns provider profiles into provisioned accounts.

nodes.label: OAuth Provider Server
nodes.summary: Outline-as-an-OAuth-provider endpoints for issuing tokens to third-party clients.

nodes.label: Auth Provider Plugins
nodes.summary: Pluggable SSO integrations (Google, OIDC, Slack, Azure) registered at startup.

nodes.label: Collaboration Server
nodes.summary: Hocuspocus extensions that authenticate, persist, and broadcast Yjs document edits.

nodes.label: Websockets
nodes.summary: Socket.io service that pushes live entity changes and presence to connected clients.

nodes.label: Queues & Processors
nodes.summary: BullMQ queues with event processors and scheduled tasks for async work like emails and backlinks.

nodes.label: Process Services
nodes.summary: Selectable process roles (web, worker, websockets, collaboration, admin, cron).

nodes.label: Sequelize Models
nodes.summary: ORM model definitions for the core domain (Document, Collection, User, Team, Revision).

nodes.label: Document.ts
nodes.summary: The central Document model holding content, collaborative state, and tree relationships.

nodes.label: Database Connection
nodes.summary: Sequelize connection setup plus the Redis client used for caching and queues.

nodes.label: Migrations
nodes.summary: Sequelize migration scripts that evolve the Postgres schema over time.

nodes.label: Storage Core
nodes.summary: Server file-handling utilities and request-scoped storage helpers.

nodes.label: Storage Plugin (Local/S3)
nodes.summary: Pluggable storage backend abstracting local disk and S3-compatible object stores.

steps.description: User hits the Login scene and selects an SSO provider.
The auth route redirects to the provider and handles the callback.
The provider plugin (e.g. Google/OIDC) returns the authenticated profile.
The Passport callback normalizes the profile and triggers provisioning.
accountProvisioner finds or creates the Team and User records.
User/Team/AuthenticationProvider rows are persisted via Sequelize.
A signed session cookie is issued and the user is redirected into the app.
The Document scene mounts the editor for a new or existing document.
DocumentsStore issues an API call to create or update the document.
documents.ts validates input and runs middleware/policy checks.
Authorization confirms the user may create/edit in the target collection.
documentCreator/updater performs the transactional write.
The Document model persists content and metadata to Postgres.
The saved document is serialized back to JSON for the client.
Async processors handle backlinks, notifications, and search indexing.
The client editor connects to the collaboration websocket for a document.
AuthenticationExtension verifies the user's access to the document.
Yjs updates are applied against the shared ProseMirror schema.
PersistenceExtension debounces and writes the merged state back.
The Document model stores the collaborative state and text.
Change and presence events are broadcast to other connected clients.

sections.title: Overview
sections.body_markdown:
## Overview

Outline is a team knowledge base and collaborative wiki. Users write rich documents organized into collections, edit them together in real time, and find them through full-text search.

The codebase is a TypeScript monorepo split into three roots:

- **`app/`** — the React + MobX single-page application (the Frontend App Shell and client editor).
- **`server/`** — the Koa HTTP server, JSON API, background workers, and the collaboration server.
- **`shared/`** — isomorphic code used by both, most importantly the ProseMirror editor schema.

Reusable integrations (SSO providers, storage backends, third-party services) live under **`plugins/`** and are registered at startup.

sections.title: Architecture
sections.body_markdown:
## Architecture

The browser runs the **Frontend App Shell**: scenes render UI, **MobX stores** hold observable state and call the API, and **client models** mirror server entities.

Server-side, a single binary can run one of several **process services** (web, worker, websockets, collaboration, cron). The **web** service exposes the **API Layer** — versioned routes guarded by **middlewares**, authorized by **policies**, executing **commands** for writes, and shaping responses through **presenters**.

State lives in Postgres via **Sequelize models**, with Redis backing caches and **BullMQ queues**. Real-time editing runs through a **Hocuspocus collaboration** server that persists Yjs state, while **Socket.io websockets** broadcast entity and presence changes.

sections.title: Key Flows
sections.body_markdown:
## Key Flows

**User login (SSO/OAuth).** The Login scene redirects through an auth provider plugin; the Passport callback provisions a Team and User via commands and issues a session cookie.

**Create & edit a document.** The Document scene calls the API through DocumentsStore; `documents.ts` runs policy checks, a command performs the write to the Document model, and queue processors handle backlinks, notifications, and indexing.

**Real-time collaboration.** The client editor opens a collaboration socket; the Hocuspocus AuthenticationExtension authorizes it, Yjs updates apply against the shared schema, PersistenceExtension writes state back, and websockets broadcast changes to peers.

sections.title: Entry Points
sections.body_markdown:
## Entry Points

- **Client:** `app/index.tsx` boots the React tree and routing.
- **Server:** `server/index.ts` selects which **process services** to run and starts them.
- **HTTP/API:** the web service mounts `server/routes/api/index.ts`; auth flows mount at `server/routes/auth/index.ts`.
- **Real-time:** the collaboration and websockets services start from `server/services/collaboration.ts` and `server/services/websockets.ts`.
- **Background work:** the worker service drains queues defined in `server/queues/index.ts`.

sections.title: Data Model
sections.body_markdown:
## Data Model

The domain is defined by **Sequelize models** in `server/models/`. Core entities:

- **Team** — the top-level workspace/tenant.
- **User** with **UserAuthentication** / **AuthenticationProvider** — accounts and their SSO links.
- **Collection** — a folder grouping documents, with its own permissions.
- **Document** — the central entity, holding rich-text content, collaborative state, and parent/child tree relationships; revisions are tracked in **Revision**.
- **Attachment**, **Comment**, **Share**, **Star**, **Subscription**, **Event** — supporting entities.

Schema changes are applied through **migrations** in `server/migrations/`, and the connection is configured in `server/storage/database.ts`.

sections.title: Getting Started
sections.body_markdown:
## Getting Started

1. Copy `.env.sample` to `.env` and configure Postgres, Redis, a storage backend, and at least one auth provider.
2. Install dependencies with `yarn` (the repo uses Yarn workspaces).
3. Run `yarn dev` to start the Vite client and the Node server with hot reload.
4. The client entry is `app/index.tsx`; the server entry is `server/index.ts`.
5. Add or edit rich-text features in the **shared editor schema** under `shared/editor/`, which both the client editor and the collaboration server consume.
6. New integrations (SSO, storage, notifications) are added as **plugins** under `plugins/`.
